title: Dense Ukrainian Vegetarian Pilaf
description: A hearty and flavorful pilaf featuring tender vegetables and a rich tomato sauce, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a large saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped carrots, zucchini, and red bell pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender, about 15 minutes.
Stir in the crushed tomatoes, vegetable broth, tomato paste, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper. Bring to a simmer.
Transfer the saucepan to the preheated oven and cook, covered, for 20-25 minutes, until the pilaf is tender and the sauce has thickened.
While the pilaf cooks, prepare the sous-vide machine according to the manufacturer's instructions.
About 10-15 minutes before serving, place the brown rice in the sous-vide machine and vacuum-seal. Set the temperature to 170°F (77°C) and cook for 1 hour.
Once the pilaf is cooked, fluff it with a fork and stir in the cooked brown rice.
Serve the pilaf hot, topped with a pat of unsalted butter and any desired additional garnishes.
